In this webinar we have Ulrike Raidl, kicking off the multi-day webinar series. In it, we discuss an array of updates for the 2023 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (FPS0). We delve into the SAP product strategy, the delivery models for Cloud ERP, RISE packaging, the evolution of SAP S/4HANA alongside highlighting significant changes in various lines of business like procurement, asset management and finance. Additionally, new features are coming our way such as integration with Microsoft Teams, an inbuilt collaboration tool, and strategies for enhanced sustainability. 

We also have a glimpse into the future of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Developments to look forward to includes smarter methods for goods receipt, inventive work orders, enhancements in shipping and receiving, along with tools to monitor our carbon footprint. In broader terms, by 2025 we have a planned shift towards central business configuration for SAP S/4HANA installation. Finally, we would like to emphasize that we value your input and are open to suggestions you can provide to SAP using the influence programs. 

SAP portfolio strategy 

Looking on SAP's priority, the Cloud ERP portfolio is based on our business technology platform (SAP BTP) and incorporates innovation and sustainability. SAP offers industry-specific solutions, along with essential business portfolios like HR, spend management, and CRM. The two primary Cloud ERP products are 'GROW with SAP', a standardized public offering for organizations that are on a growth trajectory and happy to work with a highly standardized offering, and 'RISE with SAP', a customizable and adaptable solution tailored to fit unique business needs that helps organizations continue to run on their standards. 

RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition today 

'RISE with SAP' is geared to boost your digital transformation and cloud migration. This private edition offering is tailored to various needs and comes with a single central contract and one Service Level Agreement (SLA). Then, we'll get a sneak peek at the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and a toolkit for digital transformation, loaded with the necessary infrastructure, security, resources, and migration and adoption services. We'll look at the evolution of SAP S/4HANA since it beginnings in 2015. Lastly, we'll find out about the extended seven-year release strategy and constant innovation promised with SAP S/4HANA. 

2023 Highlights Overview 

We explore the diverse features of 'RISE with SAP' and their 'Premium Plus Edition', which offers additional capabilities for generative AI, sustainability, advanced suite for The Office of the Chief Financial Officer (OCFO) and access to supplier networks. For example, in the area of procurement, we'll delve into how the recent improvements can shape our sustainability efforts and keep track of our carbon footprint. Or, in the area of manufacturing, we'll get to discover the new advanced scheduling board for production orders. Or, in the Cross Topics area, we'll look at how Microsoft Teams integrated with an in-house Collaboration Manager feature boost our teamwork and communication. 

Outlook on upcoming innovations 

We'll get a sneak peek into what's in the pipeline for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, including boosting usability, intelligent scenarios leveraging our digital assistant Joule and significant investment in reporting on green data with our carbon accounting innovation . Plus, customers can check out the product roadmap that shows what's next for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, what new things are coming up, and what their big ideas are for 2025. From the Co-Innovation Program, Continuous Influence Program, to Customer Engagement Initiatives, Beta Testing, Integration Experience Feedback, and User Groups, Early Adopter Care, can genuinely shape innovation's path forward. 

Other Webinars in the Series

  • Overview 
  • Sales
  • Finance
  • Research and Development (includes the area formerly known as "Project Systems" - PS)
  • Supply Chain
  • Sourcing and Procurement
  • Manufacturing 
  • Asset Management
  • Service

 

SAP S/4HANA SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Sales
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary

In this webinar, we have Christine Hofmann, Solution Expert of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, offering a well-rounded review of the 2023 release of S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in Sales. Let's have a look at how sales is evolving, interesting things about solution quotations and SAP's billing and revenue management, and how a customer can have a say in SAP's development plans. 

We first explore updates in sales, focusing on role-based access for pricing elements, improved product returns, Fiori apps updates, and the use of spreadsheets for contract uploads. We also discuss changes in available-to-promise (ATP) results and upcoming platform extensions. Then, we look at solution quotations with a new multi-level configuration. In the SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management section, we discuss advancements in Subscription Order Management, Contract Accounting, and Convergent Invoicing. Finally, we glimpse SAP's roadmap and see how we can influence the future of the products. 

Key features and enhancements 

  1. Role-Based Access Controls: Introducing accurate access controls to price and cost details in sales and billing documents. This feature safeguards the business from information misuse, delivering relevant data access to authorized staff, and preventing unnecessary access. 

  2. Flexible Customer Return Procedures: With the 2023 release, we can use a return product while the valuation process is pending, enhancing the user experience and ensuring a smooth and easy return process. 

  3. Sales Contract Creation via Spreadsheet: Simplifying sales contract management, the 2023 release enables creation of sales contracts through spreadsheet uploads, saving time and manual entry efforts. 

  4. Advanced Configuration in Solution Quotations: The update brings a new feature that lets us set up solution quotations for subscription products with multi-level advanced variant configuration. Many innovations also let us see info about price groups, subscription billing, plan cycles, and Work Breakdown Structure elements (WBS elements) right in our solution quotations. 

  5. Refined ATP Analysis in Sales Order: With this 2023 release, we enable the analysis and adoption of confirmed delivery dates and quantities on the Availability-To-Promise (ATP) results screen for sales-order items in the SAP Fiori app. 

  6. Expansion of BRIM: There are new features in subscription order management like adding project breakdown elements, setting payment deadlines, adjusting refunds, and support for multiple configurations. Upgrades in contract accounting and convergent invoicing include longer contract periods, linking payment functions, a more adaptable approval flow, and more flexibility for billing plans.
     
  7. Future Roadmap: The SAP Roadmap provides a comprehensive view of the planned enhancements and features for future releases, painting a vivid picture of where SAP aims to take its products.

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Finance
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary  

In this webinar, we have Michel Haesendonckx discussing the strategic product direction, and planned and delivered innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 in the Finance line of business.

Key features, enhancements and roadmap items 

  1. Financial Planning & Analysis: This process supports strategic planning by allowing ad-hoc analysis and providing insights into costs and margins to understand financial performance. This increases confidence in business plans with the help of reliable data. 

  2. Margin Reporting: This feature allows us to track down details not only on Profit & Loss but also on specific balance sheet items. 

  3. Dashboards: There are enhancements in the delivery of complex dashboards that combine information from different sources. These dashboards are designed based on finance foundation in SAP Datasphere directly integrated to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, to provide more robust financial content from different areas.  

  4. SAP S/4HANA Finance Foundation: This feature provides an end-to-end finance foundation within the SAP Datasphere application, enabling the combination of information from different sources, even those residing outside the S/4HANA system. 

  5. Flexibility in Defining Market Segments: The new release offers more flexibility in defining and updating market segments, independent of the operating concern. 

  6. Profit Center Valuations: The additional ledger for profit center valuation, pricing agreements between profit centers, and the elimination of intercompany margins, allows profit centers to trade at arm's length using transfer pricing. 

  7. Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA): The UPA is a key innovation that extends the ledger concept not only to financial accounting but also to controlling. With UPA, we can establish a parallel value flow across end-to-end processes. 

  8. Carbon Accounting: This is a pioneering innovation that allows transactional carbon accounting within the S/4HANA system as part of the Universal Journal. This is part of the Green Ledger approach where sustainability information is treated similarly to financial data. 

  9. Record to Report: This process ensures that every financial accounting entry is event-based, resulting in reduced manual intervention and improved financial reporting. 

  10. Financial Closing: Enhancements have been made in defining validation rules and the automation of certain tasks in the financial closing process.
     
  11. Enterprise Service Management: This is a next-generation solution supporting end-to-end shared services automation with Generative AI, making it particularly useful for Shared Service Organizations. 

  12. International Trade and Global Tax Management: The release also includes enhancements in trade compliance, tax compliance, and documentary reporting compliance. 

  13. Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC): There's a focus on integrating risk management and financial information, facilitated by SAP Financial Compliance Management solution.

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Research and Development
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording. 

Summary

In this webinar, we have Vinay V, discussing the latest release highlights of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 in the Research and Development (R&D) line of business. It is a broad area that includes project control (including project logistics and project financial control – classically the area that Project Systems covered), and product engineering as the core areas. R&D also includes product compliance, product lifecycle management (PLM), portfolio and project management, and circular economy. The 2023 release brings improvements in many of these areas. 

Key features and enhancements 

  1. Project Control: With the 2023 release project cash management is made to acces the Universal Journal directly. Also, new integration of responsibility management into project systems enables efficient team assignment for workflow and situation handling, etc. 

  2. Product Development Foundation: A new item category called a 'non-plant item' has been introduced to add services and project items without needing a plant assignment. It simplifies the set-up, especially in service-based organizations. 

  3. Change Management: Taking feedback from customers, we have provided the ability to add the same component multiple times with different relevancy into a change record. This is useful especially for long-running change processes, providing transparency over different phases of product change. 

  4. Integration with External PLM Systems: Enhanced integration with external Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) systems is made possible through seamless bi-directional document exchange between SAP solutions to an external PLM system. 

  5. Product Compliance: In the context of substance management, better management of customer-specific phrases (“called company-owned phrases") is now available. This reduces complexity, eliminates duplicates, and ensures synchronization for effective compliance management. 

  6. Listed substances provisioning through regulatory content service: This update ensures organizations to stay informed with the latest listed substances and listed substance group assignments. 

  7. Substance Volume Tracking: Enriched control and flexibility are offered for substance volume tracking processes. This functionality helps organizations comply with regulatory requirements by recording quantities of sensitive substances. 

  8. Safety Data Sheets Handling: Enhanced handling of multiple and international addresses in safety data sheets results in efficient and regulation-compliant documentation and labelling – for example, printing of product names in English and Chinese.

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Supply Chain
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary 

In this webinar, we have Vinay V, delving into highlights of new features and enhancements included in the 2023 release of SAP S/4HANA in the supply chain line of business. From inventory management to order promising, we have covered a wide range of scope in this release. The logistics area sees advancements like composite barcode scanning support and the introduction of advanced shipping and receiving processes. For sustainability, we also introduced capabilities for emissions tracking in transportation management. The 2023 release also includes the introduction of a new business process scheduling framework and further improvements to the packaging framework. 

Key features and enhancements 

  1. Composite Barcode Scanning: We have enhanced the ability to scan barcodes to recognize composite barcodes, which contain more information than their one-dimensional ones. This includes material information, global trade information, batch information, serial information, and more. 

  2. Advanced Shipping and Receiving: We have streamlined end-to-end processes for transporting and warehousing products. The integration of embedded warehouse management and transportation management within a single system has simplified many logistics processes. There's also updated integration to Defects and Notifications in Decentralized SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM). 

  3. Unified Package Builder: The unified package builder for handling packaging material has been further enhanced, making it consumable in the context of core logistics for both outbound and inbound deliveries. 

  4. Warehouse Management with Robotics: The integration of warehouse management with a native cloud-based solution called Warehouse Robotics is a significant development in warehouse automation. In larger warehouses, this can be extended to fleet management systems. 

  5. Sustainability in Transportation Management: There’s a new ability to track greenhouse gas emissions in the area of transportation. This helps support sustainability initiatives while providing clear indications of emission performance. 

  6. Order Promising with Availability Change Log (ACL): The availability change log allows changes in supply and demand to be factored into order promising. This provides better order promising capabilities for customers. 

  7. Business Process Scheduling: A new logistics-based scheduling framework, considering different scheduling algorithms used in transportation management, is introduced. This can provide better predictability of material availability.

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Sourcing and Procurement
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary

In this session, we have Michael Deller, taking a deep dive with us into what's new with the 2023 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in the sourcing and procurement line of business. We go through an overview of the functional areas of Sourcing and Procurement, then we zoom in on the latest innovations, all tailored to boost productivity of your operations - from central procurement to product sourcing, and beyond. The innovations include the capability for mass creation or modification of model product specifications, improved accuracy in supplier evaluations through a plant-level factory calendar, and simplification of product sourcing via a dedicated overview page. Additionally, the update also focuses on sustainability with the ability for suppliers to submit carbon footprints. The integration of AI for material group recommendations and provisions for mass changes to centrally managed purchase orders using the SAP Fiori app also adds to the efficiency. Usability enhancements in self-service requisitioning streamline various aspects. We will also refer to SAP Road Map Explorer that helps to get informed of the future of our product road map.

Key features and enhancements

  1. Mass Creation of Model Product Specifications: This new app allows users to import model product specifications using spreadsheets, easily create or modify model product specifications, including up to five levels of item hierarchies that can include products, services, and free text.

  2. Supplier Evaluations: We have added the ability to maintain a factory calendar on the plant level, allowing users to define working and nonworking days more accurately. With this update, nonworking days will no longer be considered for supplier evaluation. Furthermore, you will be able to configure the factory calendar for the calculation of supplier evaluation scores. This enhancement increases the accuracy of calculating supplier evaluation scores for time variance.

  3. Product Sourcing Overview Page: The new features allow sourcing managers to collaborate with stakeholders throughout the sourcing process. They can now easily track and review various sourcing projects and associated quotations with the new overview app.

  4. Collecting and Managing Carbon Footprint Data from Suppliers During Direct Material Sourcing Process: Sourcing managers create  cost breakdown templates, mark relevant materials, let suppliers provide cost breakdown information during bids, and compare this information to determine the best supplier bid. Beyond quantities and qualities, collect carbon related data from your suppliers in the same well-structured way.

  5. AI Material Group Recommendations: Get proposals of recommended material groups with confidence scores based on a machine learning algorithm that analyzes previous purchase orders.

  6. Mass Changes of Procurement: Mass changes of centrally managed purchase requisitions can now be done by using SAP Fiori app in online mode rather than just via Excel uploads.

  7. Usability Enhancements in Self-Service Requisitioning: Numerous smaller updates have been made to improve self-service requisitioning such as Improved Status Tracking, Storage Location, and Ability to Copy Accounting Details.

  8. Road Map Explorer: This is a centrally accessible source for all future development plans for SAP products. The road map explorer allows you to filter depending on product, line of business, which industry or any other criterion relevant to you.

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Manufacturing
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary

In this webinar on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Vinay V and Stefan Gerber explore highlights of new features with release 2023 in the Manufacturing line of business. The discussion covers a range of areas, all meant to streamline our operations from planning to the shop floor. Exciting enhancements include Order Notes Management, Interactive Scheduling Board, and improved Capacity Planning. Meanwhile, managing inputs from multiple storage tanks becomes easier with Tank Planning, and the Time-Dependent Safety Stock and Reorder Point is there to handle seasonality and special situations for report-point replenishment of stocked materials. There are improvements to Advanced Scheduling Board  for smoother planning and rescheduling.

Key Features and Enhancements

  1. Order Notes Management: Improves hand-over from production planning to the shop floor by structuring the notes based on planned orders or in-house production orders.

  2. Interactive Scheduling Board: Enables resource assignment through simple drag and drop, considering all dependencies and constraints.

  3. Capacity Planning Enhancements: Provides updated functionalities including the grouping of work centers, displaying the sales order for made-to-order scenarios, and improvement to the Capacity Scheduling Table app to display the availability date of components.

  4. Tank Planning for Process Manufacturing: Can manage inputs from multiple storage tanks along with a flexible definition of storage tank quantities and their sequencing and the intuitive Fiori UI.

  5. Time-Dependent Safety Stock and Reorder Point: Provides flexibility to define and handle special situations, as well as phases for temporary adjustment of inventory levels like campaigns or seasonal trends, without the need for continuous updates of the master data.

  6. Advanced Scheduling Board: Adds a new interactive Gantt chart that allows for a flexible evaluation of the planning horizon, with the added ability to reschedule and realign interactively. Furthermore, the scope of responsibility now includes work center groups.

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Asset Management
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary

In this webinar, we have Noboru Ota, exploring the latest additions and improvements in the Asset Management line of business with the new 2023 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. The priorities for the 2023 release are to assist businesses in achieving a closed loop between asset strategy and execution and to  further enhance the core maintenance management capabilities.

For user experience, we will see system demonstrations on the new features added to mass editing, work packs printing, object lists modifications, and equipment structure configurations. Furthermore, we will look at significant improvements to the work permit process, with new Fiori apps simplifying user interactions to enhance safety measures and precautions throughout the maintenance procedure.

Key Features and Improvements

Key features and enhancements include:

  1.  Mass Editing: We have added mass editing capabilities to an assortment of Fiori apps so that we can edit numerous entries simultaneously, providing a faster and more streamlined way to manage data.

  2. Printing and Output of Work Packs: We can now print multiple shop papers and job cards in one go. You can flexibly group them by various criteria such as work center, maintenance technician, or the technical object being maintained or repaired, and include attachments as part of the printout. This includes auto-attachment based on output and document types.

  3. Adding Object List Entries to Maintenance Items: With the ability to add, remove, or change object list entries within maintenance items, we can have better control and efficiency in managing data.

  4. Configuration of Equipment Structure: An easy way to configure the equipment structure. This feature includes the ability to install new components or dismantle existing ones and record the action details.

  5. Work Permit (Work Clearance Management): With the new Fiori apps, the work permit process is simplified, while still ensuring a safe work environment. This process can be customized as per the organization's needs and uses predefined templates for recurring safety measures. We can link a permit to maintenance orders and use the work permit for search and display it in the resultant list of maintenance orders.

entry.title: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Service
entry.content:
Click on the image or here to view the webinar video recording.

Summary

In this webinar, we have Noboru Ota, exploring how the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 release enhances the service line of business. We will learn about highlights of improvements in service management capabilities in the new release in system demonstrations. The highlights include the new Item-Based Accounting, which provides real-time visibility into profitability and revenues for your service business. New features also cover complex asset maintenance processes with the Service with Advanced Execution and scenarios like inter-company service execution.

Key Features and Improvements

  1. Improved Visibility into Profitability and Revenues Recognition: This new financial integration feature (Item-Based Accounting) offers real-time cost and revenue recording and analysis for service orders and contracts, enhancing reporting and improving margin analysis. It also eliminates the need for period ending activities and data warehouse tools, providing real-time profitability for your service business.

  2. Service with Advanced Execution: This feature offers a full-service process to be divided into a commercial service order and an execution maintenance order, facilitating detailed planning and quote generation based on the simulated actuals. Introducing Configurable Task Lists reduce work planning time by describing a sequence of repeat service activities. Furthermore, we now have a system that supports Resource-Related Billing using a Dynamic Item Processor profile (DIP), transforming actual service costs into billable items.

  3. Inter-company Service Execution: This feature facilitates the modelling of service business procedures, allows different companies to complete various aspects of the service execution process, and automates internal cost transfers.

Other Webinars in the Series

  • Overview 
  • Sales
  • Finance
  • Research and Development (includes the area formerly known as "Project Systems" - PS)
  • Supply Chain
  • Sourcing and Procurement
  • Manufacturing 
  • Asset Management
  • Service

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management Best Practices with SAP BTP
entry.content:
In this excerpt from the podcast “SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management best practices”, Terry Penner, Bill Todd and Randy Connelly discuss best practices and practical approaches for migrating data and master data management with SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Technology Platform.

Topics discussed

Part 1: Data Migration

  • Key challenges
  • Best practices for system conversion
  • Best practices for selective data transition
  • Best practices for new implementations
  • Data migration tool options

Part 2: Master Data Management

  • Challenges and priorities
  • Efficiently working with master data

Part 3: Clean Core

  • Data management and clean core
  • Getting started with data migration to S/4HANA

About the speakers

Terry Penner is part of the SAP BTP Marketing and Solutions team, focused on BTP for SAP S/4HANA. He has more than 20 years experience in the SAP platform and analytics, including working directly on hundreds of customer implementations.

Bill: is a principal solution advisor from SAP.  For the past 20 years, his focus has been on data management solutions.  He is passionate about data, and has deep experience in data quality, data integration and master data management.

Randy: is the global data governance practice lead for Syniti, with more than 30 years experience in the SAP space, in data migration and data quality.  The last 12 years have been focused on master data governance.

Part 1: Data Migration

Terry: Welcome to our expert panel discussion on best practices for migrating data and managing master data for S/4HANA cloud customers, particularly those in the early stages of their RISE with SAP journey.

Let's start off talking about data migration.  First, why is data migration and master data management important to an S/4HANA customer starting their RISE journey.

Bill: Accurate and trusted master data is crucial for any business initiative, particularly for transitioning to S/4HANA and embarking on your RISE journey. Without a solid foundation of reliable data, you risk building on unstable ground due to potential inaccuracies. To draw an analogy, think of constructing a building - everything begins with a strong foundation. Similarly, master data forms the fundamental base for S/4HANA.

Randy: Migration is a critical component of your digital transformation into S/4 HANA. This process involves setting up key business standards, policies, and rules to meet your operational needs.   On top of that, these standards are leveraged in the master data governance area. This is to ensure that you maintain data integrity by consistently applying the standards, policies, and rules set during migration.

As long as organizations utilize these standards, they can improve their operations and efficiencies. This will also pave the way for future innovations.

 

Key data migration challenges

Terry: Based on your experience with customers, what are some of the key data challenges that S/4HANA customers are facing as they migrate to S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, whether it's from ERP or S/4HANA on-premises?

Randy: One thing I often notice is the crucial role of data cleansing, harmonization, and aligning business interests. By alignment, I mean getting everyone on board, whether on a regional or global level, with new standards, policies, and rules. Achieving this consensus can be tricky due to differing viewpoints. So, it's essential to focus on change management to ensure solid user acceptance and confidence, paving the way for smooth progress.

Bill:  Absolutely, Randy, I totally agree. It all circles back to the quality of data. We really don't want to carry over old data issues into the new system. This is our opportunity to get it right, and data quality is always an issue that needs careful handling.

As for the S/4HANA customers you asked about, I've found that the conversion of business partner data is a significant and often tricky task. In S/4HANA, we see our customers and vendors through the business partner model. It's a broad category that encompasses companies that might be both a customer and a vendor.

The challenge here lies in the transition of data. Currently, most companies treat customers and vendors as separate entities. But with S/4HANA, they need to be viewed as business partners. This is a critical aspect that needs addressing as part of the migration process. Moving forward, we will treat such records as business partners.

 

Data migration best practices for system conversion

Terry: There’s three main data migration approaches to consider: system conversion, selective data transition, and a new implementation.  Let’s talk about the benefits and drawbacks of each of those options and best practises.

The first approach of system conversion, or 'lift and shift', is a popular method when moving to S/4HANA. It's basically a technical upgrade where you're doing the bare minimum. You're not really taking advantage of the new S/4HANA innovations, and there's usually little customization involved. You're not changing the data, just moving it.

What needs to be considered with system conversion?

Bill: In my experience, the approach we're discussing is ideal for companies with strong data management practices and high-quality data. It's particularly useful if you're in a rush to transition to S/4HANA and are considering a simple "lift and shift" strategy. However, ensure your existing data is of high quality to maximize the benefits of this approach. Otherwise, you're just transferring all your old data and its associated issues. If you plan on addressing data issues later, it's akin to moving all your old stuff into a new house and then deciding to declutter. It doesn't make sense, right? Why would you want to move boxes of unwanted items from your old house to your new one?

Randy: Sure, you made some excellent points there. Many organizations, especially when transitioning to S/4HANA, really start to consider implementing formal governance. Even if they're just doing a straightforward system conversion, they need to think about this in a wider context. As they begin to formalize their governance, there's likely to be changes in their standards, policies, and procedures that need to be consistently applied. This might also be a part of the 'house cleaning' process where they need to adjust their master data to align with the new processes and the data they want to use moving forward. So, the house cleaning aspect is critical in this context.

 

Data migration best practices for selective data transition

Terry: Let's talk about the second approach to migration, selective data transition.
This can involve multiple SAP ERP systems or ERP systems. Typically, there's data harmonization and cleansing required, and definitely data migration necessary.  What best practices do we need to know?

Randy: One of the key things to remember is that SAP provides applications such as ADMM [ SAP Advanced Data Migration and Management Tool ] and SKP [ Syniti Knowledge Platform ] via Syniti. These are essential for conducting important activities. An integral part of this process is the use of an application that can standardize tasks and create a process that can be repeated and reused.

Many organizations approach this on a case-by-case basis, focusing on a single ERP system, division, or business unit at a time. This repeatable and reusable process is crucial for efficiency. It allows for data to be gathered into a common repository and transformed.

Just as important is validation, which ensures alignment with the new S/4 environment. This leads to the creation of loads for the new S/4 environment, guaranteeing high quality data.

This process pairs well with formalized governance from an MDG perspective. It allows the creation of load programs that can be used for both S/4 and MDG, whether in a hub model or co-deploy back into S/4.

Bill:  When Randy mentioned the importance of a repeatable and reusable approach, he was really emphasizing the need to tackle tasks one at a time. This could mean focusing on one business unit today and another tomorrow. Or, if you often merge with or acquire companies, you'll eventually have to integrate their ERP systems.

Having a standard methodology - complete with tools, processes, rules, and data conversion routines - is crucial. This way, we can hope for a rinse-and-repeat scenario where we can apply this approach again and again. This approach allows for an effective phased implementation.

As Randy noted, this methodology often dictates how you'll actively manage and govern your data as you create it. For instance, as you generate new master data for your HANA system, you'll likely reuse many of these rules. If a rule helps keep data clean from the get-go, it's likely a good rule to keep for future data creation. Therefore, having a repeatable and reusable system is key.

 

Data migration best practices for new implementations

Terry: Yes, that will certainly help with the total cost of ownership.
And then for the third approach of starting with a new implementation, what are your thoughts on some best practices for this approach?

Randy:  Similar to what we just discussed; a key part of the process often involves a "Big Bang" implementation. This means that instead of a phased approach where each ERP system or business unit is handled individually, everything is implemented all at once.

In such a case, it's crucial to have a standardized process that can be used across all areas - be it materials, business partners, financials, or transactional data. All these must be realigned to fit into the new S/4 environment.

An aspect we didn't touch on earlier is that there might be a need for data construction. This involves creating new data that didn't exist in the old systems but is necessary in the new system to meet operational needs.

We need to leverage standardized processes to validate and create load files consistently, as there will be several test cycles. The goal is to ensure that everything is working and improving, so that by the time you reach the production implementation, you have clean data with an accuracy rate of 99% to 100%.

The drawback is the time and effort up front, but the outcome can make it well worth the effort.

Bill: The “Big Bang” approach is no small task. You're diving into a large project from the start, but the payoff is in the final product. Yes, it requires quite a bit of effort, but the result? You've tackled all the issues and managed all the data. There's hardly anything left to do when you're migrating. Well, unless you're acquiring a company or something like that.

Then, you might lean more towards the second approach we've discussed, the system conversion or selective data transition. And remember, these methods aren't always separate. Sometimes, there's a bit of a hybrid, right? They don't have to be mutually exclusive.

 

Data migration tool options

Terry: What are recommended tool options for migrating data?

Randy:  When you think about transitioning to S/4HANA, the first thing that usually comes to mind is the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit. This tool allows you to extract, map, and load data directly into your S/4HANA system. However, the key thing to consider, as we've often discussed, is the cleanliness of your data. The Migration Cockpit works wonderfully for those who've already cleaned, harmonized, and deduplicated their data.

If your data isn't clean or if you're dealing with a different situation, another option we've discussed is SAP's Advanced Data Migration by Syniti. This tool offers a comprehensive and customizable solution for your unique circumstances, regardless of whether you're migrating from multiple or single ERP systems, or a combination of SAP and non-SAP systems. It provides immense flexibility for data cleansing, harmonization, and validation, which are crucial when introducing new master or transactional data into your S/4 environment.

Part 2: Master data management – challenges and priorities

Terry: Let's pivot to master data management and some of the key challenges and priorities.

Bill: Dealing with data issues is always a significant challenge. It's crucial to identify and correct these problems. Take duplicate data, for instance. Identifying duplicates can be tricky, but many robust matching engines and techniques can help with this.

The bigger challenge I've noticed from my interactions with customers isn't identifying the duplicates but figuring out how to create the best record from these duplicates. They often refer to this process as survivorship or merging. In SAP master data governance, we call it creating a 'best record'.

The goal is to allow data experts or stewards to review the duplicate records and decide how to consolidate them. For instance, if we have five versions of the same company, we need to figure out how to create that one superior, or 'golden' record, moving forward.

A solution like SAP Master Data Governance can be helpful in this scenario because it has specialized processes, tasks, and user interfaces for reviewing duplicate records and creating a 'best' or 'golden' record. So, that's one major challenge. But there are others, of course.

Apart from correcting data issues, another significant challenge that comes to mind is automation. In this era of AI, companies want to automate as much as possible, like auto-creating master data or extending materials to plants and sales organizations. This has been a focus in the last two releases of SAP master data governance with its data derivation capabilities. With the current trend of generative AI, I foresee a lot of progress in the future in this area. So the two critical areas are automation and correcting data issues.

Randy: A key term that's often linked to this discussion is self-enablement. Many organizations are striving for this, to reduce their dependence on IT departments. They're leveraging platforms like MDG to empower the business to maintain data quality, ensuring they have reliable data they can trust.

You gave an example of an international manufacturer of industrial materials. They faced issues during the deduplication process due to variations in units of measure - metric or imperial. This problem affected warranties and spare parts for their products across different regions.

A product purchased in one region may end up in another, but the warranties still apply. However, when spare parts are needed, if there's a discrepancy between metric and imperial measurements, it becomes a significant international challenge.

To overcome this, they reviewed their governance process, looking at how they could standardize units of measure globally. This ensures they can support their products anywhere in the world.

This situation demonstrates the importance of master data. It's not just about the top-level data - the underlying attributes also need to be analyzed. In the context of data governance, it's crucial to understand how these are used and the impact they have on the business.

 

Efficiently working with master data

Terry: What are key points that I need to know about working with master data efficiently in S/4HANA?

Bill: When discussing master data management and S/4HANA, it's crucial to stress the importance of establishing appropriate governance early on for creating or altering master data for S/4. If not, you run the risk of S/4HANA reverting to past behaviors, potentially operating in a system that didn't uphold governance over master data creation.

That's why companies transitioning to S/4HANA should think about using something like SAP master data governance. I often describe it as a gatekeeper, or even a bouncer, acting like a firewall to ensure we stick to a regulated process. This process actively governs how we create or update master data, ensuring it's accurate, reliable, consistent, and compliant with any regulations. Plus, it provides transparency so we can trace how that master data was created.

Without such governance, you could end up creating master data without any checks, validations, or oversight, and without an audit trail. That's where MDG steps in, providing a governance layer to control how master data is created for S/4HANA.

And don't delay - many times, if you wait until you're live on S/4HANA, it's almost too late. On day one, people will start creating master data, so if you don't have this in place from the start, you're setting yourself up for potential difficulties.

Randy:  I agree with you Bill, especially when transitioning to S/4 it's essential to establish formal governance. This isn't just about cleaning up your data, but also maintaining its cleanliness.

There are many organizations currently mapping out their transition strategies and they might not be ready just yet. However, they can utilize governance and SAP MDG as stepping stones towards S/4. A lot of organizations might not realize that they can set up an MDG environment on S/4HANA, which allows them to introduce formal data governance into their legacy ECC or ERP systems.

While preparing to transition to S/4, this approach gives them the data model necessary for the S/4 environment and accommodates new data requirements. This data can then be replicated back to existing ECC and non-SAP systems in their current formats, keeping the data clean.

Once they activate their S/4 environment, they already have all the necessary information prepared and it's compatible with the new S/4 data models. So there are really some excellent options for organizations looking to clean their data and transition to S/4.

 

Part 3: Data management and clean core

Terry:  How does data migration and fit in with a clean core strategy and even more important, what happens after I migrate the data.  How do I how do I stay clean?

Bill: It's important to remember that "clean core" can have different meanings for different people, depending on the context. In this case, when we're talking about migrating data to S/4HANA, a clean core refers to the accuracy and usability of that data. Everyone wants a clean core system, and that always begins with clean data.

Randy: Organizations aiming to transition to a clean core will need to strategize on how they'll undertake data cleaning, harmonization, and migration activities. They'll face challenges, but they'll also have to determine how they're going to map eliminated data elements to the new clean core system. This is crucial to ensure business activities continue to run smoothly. They will need to consider the migration issues we discussed earlier as part of their shift to a clean core.


Terry: Yes, we recently did a podcast and blog on a deeper dive on what clean core means, whether it's extensions, processes, data - it's an important concept.  How does how does MDM master data management fit into that clean core strategy?

Bill: For the importance of maintaining cleanliness in your systems, we emphasize the need to clean up data, migrate it to S/4, and then continue to keep it clean.

That's precisely where master data management becomes crucial. It's a process that helps you maintain this clean core system. As I've mentioned, active governance and control are key. You need the right people, process, and technology to ensure your core stays clean. This is where master data management, specifically solutions like SAP master data governance, come into play.

Randy: Earlier we emphasized the importance of maintaining cleanliness in our system. The central idea is to first clean up the data, transition smoothly to S/4, and then ensure it stays pristine.

This is where the role of master data management becomes critical. It's a process that's essential for sustaining a clean core system. Like I mentioned before, it's about actively governing and controlling the system. To do this effectively, you need the right combination of people, processes, and technology. That's where a specific solution like SAP Master Data Governance steps in to support this goal.

 

Getting started with data migration to S/4HANA

Terry: Any final words of advice for customers who are migrating data to S/4HANA, whether they’re doing the “Big Bang” approach or a new implementation.

Randy: I think it's crucial for any organization to carefully consider what path suits them best. Every organization is unique, with its own needs and culture, especially when it comes to handling change management. Whether a company can handle a 'Big Bang' approach or if it needs to take it step by step, maybe rolling out a new ERP system or tackling it division by division to ensure success.

We've gone over many of these points in our conversation, but it really comes down to a case-by-case basis. It's about finding the right fit for each organization, and that includes deciding if a 'clean core' strategy is the right move. The big plus of a clean core is how it simplifies upgrades. It takes away a lot of the hassle and worry because, with a clean core, your upgrades are automatically in sync, whether we're talking about S/4 or your MDG platform.  There are a lot of considerations for every organization.

Bill: Clean core is essential for streamlining business processes. The key is to do things differently, to adopt best practices, and to work efficiently, making sure everything aligns with SAP's recommended methods. This includes using SAP Advanced Data Migration and Management, or ADMM, to ensure data is clean, correct, and smoothly transferred to S/4. Then, there's SAP Master Data Governance, or MDG, which is crucial for overseeing how master data is created.

I remember working with a major high-tech company, and they had a clear rule: the only way into S/4 is through SAP MDG. It's how they ensure their master data is properly set up for S/4. They made it clear to everyone in the company that this was the change they had to embrace. By sticking to this process, they could trust that their data foundation would be accurate and reliable for the future.

Terry: Thanks Bill and Randy for joining me in this conversation and discuss this important topic for S/4HANA customers.

Listen to the full podcast at SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management best practices with SAP BTP

Key links:

  • SAP BTP for RISE with SAP
  • RISE with SAP
  • SAP S/4HANA Extensions and Clean Core Best Practices
  • SAP Advanced Data Migration and Management Tool
  • SAP Master Data Governance

entry.title: Assign Accounting Principles to Ledgers and Company Code: SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Leading Ledger

SAP delivers the leading ledger 0L. This ledger is updated in all company codes, that is, it's assigned to all company codes. The document numbers assigned in this ledger apply to all dependent ledgers. In each company code, the leading ledger receives exactly the same settings that apply to that company code: the currencies, the fiscal year variant, and the variant of the posting periods. For these reasons, the leading ledger can act as the global ledger for the whole entity (that is, with the valuation done according to legal reporting requirements).

Non-Leading Ledgers

Along with the leading ledger, you can have another, non-leading ledger or extension ledger. The non-leading ledger is a parallel ledger to the leading ledger. It can be based on a local accounting principle, for example. You can either use ledger 2L (by default, for IFRS) or ledger 3L (by default, for US GAAP) as the non-leading ledger. You have to activate the non-leading ledger for the individual company codes.

If there is a mismatch in the ledger assignment as per the standard expectation, Addressing this issue will require you (Customer),

  1. To reset the data (refer to documentation) for all company codes in both DEV and TEST systems and then
  2. reassign* the local accounting principle as recommended below.

Current Settings / As is situation. (This will be the default configuration when partner receives the initial system access to CBC from SAP). FI consultant should check the configuration and do the needful.

Ledger

Corporate Accounting Principle

Local Accounting Principle

0L

IFRS

 

2L

LKAP

 

Recommended Customizing / To be situation.

Ledger

Corporate Accounting Principle

Local Accounting Principle

0L

 IFRS

 

2L

 

LKAP


*Important: Please do not create/post any fixed assets in your Production System before the recommended changes are effective in your production system. As soon as you have a transactional posting in Fixed Assets in your Production system, it will not be possible to change the Accounting Principle to Ledger settings at Company Code in your landscape.
